text: Walsh's usual boisterousness was gone. In its place was something focused and intent.

sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: I've got one more run for ye. A special spot.

sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: Deeper water. Past the bay. There's a hole out there — blue as anything ye've ever seen. The fish there are... premium. Worth ten times what ye've been hauling.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: A hole? Jeff mentioned something about a deep trench—
Walsh's jaw tightened. The veins on his weathered neck became visible.

sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: The schoolteacher talks too much. Always has.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: Is it safe out there? Some of those fish from the boil looked—
Walsh grabbed Abel's arm with that uncomfortably firm grip.

sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: Ye came here because ye had nothin'. No job, no prospects, no future. I gave ye somethin' to DO. Something that MATTERS.

sequence.speaker: Walsh
text: Now are ye a fisherman or aren't ye?
The question hung in the salt air. It wasn't really a question.
